response:
<> To replace one URL with another within a string in PHP ensure that all occurrences are replaced and that the replacement is case insensitive. Here is a working example ```php $string = 'New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

'; $oldUrl = 'http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1'; $newUrl = 'http://yahoo.com/abc1'; $pattern = '/(href="|>)(http:\/\/stackoverflow\.com\/xyz1)(<|")/i'; $replacement = '$1' . $newUrl . '$3'; $result = preg_replace($pattern, $replacement, $string); echo $result; ``` This will replace all instances of the old URL with the new one within the string. The pattern uses a regular expression to match the URL in both the href attribute and the visible text. The replacement ensures that the href and the visible text are both updated. A palindrome is a word or phrase that reads the same forwards and backwards. One example is "madam" or "racecar".
